name: Lab result confusion
purpose: Help users organize lab slips and confusion about results (including widal and malaria/typhoid panels) for clinician review — not interpretation as diagnosis.
not_allowed: Interpreting widal, malaria, typhoid, or any lab value as confirming a disease
Telling the user they have malaria, typhoid, infection, or any named condition from a slip
Prescribing treatment based on lab wording alone
Replacing the laboratory or clinician who ordered the test
Declaring results normal or abnormal without clinician context

red_flags.label: very unwell regardless of slip wording
started strong medicines from slip alone

required_questions.prompt: What test was done (name on the slip)?
Where was the test done and when?
What part of the result is confusing you?
What symptoms do you have right now?
Did a clinician order this test, or was it self-requested?
Have you started any new medicines because of this result?

ai_generated_question_policy.notes: Organize slip details and questions for a clinician; never conclude illness from numbers or reactive flags alone.

allowed_actions.title: Organize lab slip details
allowed_actions.instruction: Write test name, date, facility, and the exact words or values that confuse you — without guessing meaning.

allowed_actions.title: Link symptoms to timeline
allowed_actions.instruction: Note when symptoms started relative to when the test was done.

allowed_actions.title: Questions for clinician
allowed_actions.instruction: Prepare questions: what the test checks, how it fits your symptoms, and what follow-up is needed.

allowed_actions.title: Pause self-treatment from slip alone
allowed_actions.instruction: If you started medicines only because of the slip wording, note what you took and ask a clinician or pharmacist before continuing.
